summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of MinSU Clearance System (JWT auth, email verification, clearance workflow, file uploads, audit logs, admin user mgmt). 38/38 backend pytest cases PASSED. Frontend largely functional; identified and fixed 1 critical bug (renderClearancesList incorrectly declared async, causing 'appendChild: parameter 1 is not of type Node' and a blank Clearances page for ALL roles). Login background image, OTP UI, registration, verification, clearance creation, audit log filters, and admin user management all working.

minor.issue: Returns dev_code:null when SENDGRID_API_KEY is set; if the configured SendGrid sender is unverified, real users may never receive emails. Recommend logging delivery status or surfacing in audit log details.

allow_origins=['*'] combined with allow_credentials=True is invalid per CORS spec (browsers reject). Currently irrelevant because frontend uses same-origin /api, but tighten origins before exposing API cross-origin.
Created accounts default to email_verified=true with no password complexity check; admin/faculty passwords are accepted as plain strings of any length.

ui_bugs.issue: FIXED. Function was declared `async` but main render() does `main.appendChild(renderClearancesList())` synchronously. The Promise return triggered 'Failed to execute appendChild: parameter 1 is not of type Node', leaving the Clearances page blank for student/faculty/admin. Removed `async` keyword (load() is already invoked via setTimeout).

integration_issues.issue: After submitting a clearance, app navigates to clearances list; without the fix above the page was blank. Now resolved.

issues: Background image (.auth-bg) loads correctly with opacity 0.25 over a centered white card — matches requirement.
IP column overlaps with previous Details column in some rows (visible at 1920px).

action_items: Verify renderClearancesList fix by main agent (already applied by tester) — student/faculty/admin clearances list now renders.
Optional: tighten CORS origins (don't combine '*' with allow_credentials=True).
Optional: enforce password policy & require email verification flow for admin-created faculty accounts.
Optional: align Audit Log IP/Details columns at wide viewports.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 832 lines; consider splitting into routers (auth, clearances, admin, uploads) for maintainability.
verify_password silently falls back to legacy SHA256 if hash doesn't start with $2 — fine for migration, but log a deprecation warning when used.
Email send is fire-and-forget via asyncio.create_task without exception capture; failures only show in logs, not surfaced to admin.
/api/admin/audit-logs has no rate-limit and returns up to 200 rows; for large deployments add cursor pagination.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@minsu.edu.ph / Admin@123 (verified working). Test users created during pytest are auto-cleaned.
seed_data_creation: Per-run unique test_student_<run>, test_registrar_<run>, test_librarian_<run> users created and deleted in TestZCleanup; test clearance is also removed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers register/verify/login/me/forgot-reset/clearance CRUD/process workflow/file upload+download/stats/audit-logs/admin CRUD/authorization. Uses mongosh to read verification_code from db.users since SendGrid is configured (dev_code is null). All tests pass cleanly. The only frontend fix needed was the async keyword on renderClearancesList — already applied. Background image (.auth-bg) is at /images/minsu-bg.jpg and renders with opacity 0.25.
rca_of_the_issue: Critical UI blank-page bug RCA: function renderClearancesList was declared `async` even though it builds DOM synchronously and only kicks off load() via setTimeout. Because it's async, calling it returns a Promise, which the parent render() then passed to main.appendChild(...) — DOM appendChild rejects non-Node args with 'parameter 1 is not of type Node'. Reproduction: log in (any role) → click 'Clearances' nav item → page is blank, console shows the error. Fix applied: remove `async` keyword from function declaration.
